data.title: Flux Power Faces Loan Default, Liquidity Risks Mount
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Flux Power Holdings ( (FLUX) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 31, 2026, Flux Power Holdings, Inc. disclosed that it had failed to meet the minimum EBITDA covenant for the trailing three-month period under its existing Loan and Security Agreement with Gibraltar Business Capital, LLC, triggering an event of default on approximately $6.5 million of outstanding borrowings. The lender has kept the company’s credit line available during ongoing negotiations for an amendment or waiver, but it retains the right to restrict access or accelerate repayment, creating near-term liquidity and financing risk for Flux Power and heightening uncertainty for its stakeholders.

While Flux Power has successfully renegotiated this loan agreement in the past and remains optimistic about reaching a new accommodation, there is no assurance that new terms or a waiver will be obtained on favorable conditions or at all. If Gibraltar Business Capital exercises its default remedies, it could terminate further commitments, demand immediate repayment, and enforce its rights as a secured creditor, potentially straining the company’s operations and financial flexibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (FLUX) stock is a Hold with a $0.98 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Flux Power Holdings stock, see the FLUX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FLUX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FLUX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak TTM fundamentals (ongoing losses and negative operating/free cash flow) and bearish technicals (below all major moving averages with negative MACD). A low P/E and a notably improved quarter (first profitability, margin/expense progress and product/software roadmap) provide some support, but near-term guidance risk from a major customer capital freeze and low cash keep overall risk elevated.

To see Spark’s full report on FLUX stock, click here.

More about Flux Power Holdings

Flux Power Holdings, Inc. is a U.S. company operating in the industrial and commercial power solutions sector, focused on providing battery and energy storage systems that support business operations reliant on flexible credit facilities and working capital financing. The company’s activities and growth are closely tied to maintaining access to asset-based lending and meeting related financial covenants.

Average Trading Volume: 311,122

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $23.9M

data.title: Forian to Go Private in CEO-Led Takeover Deal
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Forian ( (FORA) ) is now available.

On April 2, 2026, Forian entered into a merger agreement under which a consortium of investors led by Chairman and CEO Max Wygod, alongside other senior executives and existing stockholders, will acquire all outstanding common shares not already owned by them for $2.17 per share in cash. The all-cash deal, which values the company’s equity at about $68 million and represents a roughly 22.6% premium to its unaffected August 22, 2025, share price, will be executed via a tender offer followed by a merger that takes Forian private, removing its stock from Nasdaq while keeping its Newtown, Pennsylvania headquarters, management team and Forian brand in place, with closing targeted for the second quarter of 2026 subject to a minimum tender and other customary conditions.

The transaction was unanimously approved by Forian’s board, following a special committee review with independent financial and legal advisors, and is fully backed by equity commitments from the investor consortium, eliminating financing risk. Governance terms include caps on company transaction expenses, a no-shop with limited fiduciary outs, and a $1.5 million termination fee plus expense reimbursement in certain scenarios, underscoring a tightly structured take-private process that secures a near-term liquidity event for public shareholders while consolidating control with existing insiders.

The most recent analyst rating on (FORA) stock is a Hold with a $2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Forian stock, see the FORA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FORA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FORA is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by reduced financial risk from an essentially debt-free balance sheet and improved 2025 free cash flow, but it is held back by ongoing losses and an unhelpful negative P/E. Technical indicators add a neutral-to-slightly-weak backdrop with no clear momentum signal.

To see Spark’s full report on FORA stock, click here.

More about Forian

Forian Inc. is a data analytics and information solutions provider focused on traditional and emerging life sciences, healthcare payers and providers, and the financial services industry. The company specializes in acquiring, integrating, normalizing and commercializing large-scale healthcare data assets to help customers optimize and measure operational, clinical and financial performance.

Average Trading Volume: 18,232

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $65.54M

For an in-depth examination of FORA stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Credit Acceptance Unveils Tech-Driven Strategy for Long-Term Growth
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Credit Acceptance ( (CACC) ) has shared an announcement.

In a letter accompanying new investor materials, Credit Acceptance’s CEO, who took the role in November 2025 after four years on the board, outlines a strategy centered on maximizing long‑term intrinsic value per share and Economic Profit while maintaining strict pricing discipline. The company is accelerating technology and AI investments in originations, data infrastructure, dealer‑facing tools, and servicing to sharpen risk assessment, reduce friction, and improve scalability, signaling a push to modernize operations for the next decade.

Management reiterates a purpose‑driven focus on underserved, non‑prime consumers, stressing that Credit Acceptance’s economics are strongest when borrowers successfully repay, build credit, and maintain vehicle ownership. The letter highlights examples of consumers and dealers whose growth and stability have been supported by the company’s financing programs and portfolio profit‑sharing model, underscoring its effort to align long‑term outcomes for dealers, consumers, and shareholders in a cyclical and operationally intensive auto finance market.

The most recent analyst rating on (CACC) stock is a Buy with a $476.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Credit Acceptance stock, see the CACC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CACC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CACC is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by financial risk from a debt-heavy balance sheet and earnings volatility, despite strong cash generation. Technicals are moderately constructive and valuation looks reasonable at ~13x earnings. Recent commentary and KPIs indicate operational resilience but ongoing share and volume pressure, while recent ABS financing events modestly improve funding flexibility and costs.

To see Spark’s full report on CACC stock, click here.

More about Credit Acceptance

Credit Acceptance Corporation is an indirect auto finance company that partners with automobile dealers to serve non‑prime and credit‑challenged consumers who struggle to secure traditional financing. The company focuses on providing access to credit for vehicle purchases through dealer‑originated retail installment contracts, using data‑driven risk models and a portfolio program that lets dealers share in long‑term contract economics.

Its business model emphasizes disciplined underwriting, long‑term intrinsic value creation, and close alignment with both dealers and consumers, particularly in underserved markets where reliable transportation supports employment and financial stability. Credit Acceptance positions itself as a differentiated, AI‑enabled, data‑rich player in the non‑prime auto finance segment, seeking durable advantages through proprietary data, servicing capabilities, and dealer‑centric programs.

Bloomia Holdings ( (TULP) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 3, 2026, Bloomia Holdings, Inc. announced preliminary results of a rights offering that began in February 2026 and expired on April 1, 2026, after a brief extension to increase stockholder participation. Eligible stockholders of record as of February 16, 2026, received non-transferable rights to buy additional common shares at $4.05 each, including an over-subscription privilege subject to availability and proration.

Based on preliminary figures, the offering is expected to generate about $12.1 million in gross proceeds, with roughly $5 million in cash and $7.1 million from conversion of existing debt, leading to the issuance of approximately 3 million new shares, pending final tabulation and settlement. Bloomia plans to use the net cash proceeds primarily to repay acquisition debt at more than a 50% discount, a move that materially reduces leverage, cuts annual interest expense, and strengthens the balance sheet, which management says should support future growth and enhance long-term shareholder value.

The most recent analyst rating on (TULP) stock is a Hold with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bloomia Holdings stock, see the TULP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TULP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TULP is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by high financial risk (heavy leverage and ongoing cash burn) despite improving operating performance. Technical indicators are neutral-to-weak with oversold signals but limited momentum confirmation, and valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on TULP stock, click here.

More about Bloomia Holdings

Bloomia Holdings, Inc. is a specialty agriculture company focused on making and managing agricultural investments in the U.S. and internationally. The Nasdaq-listed firm is the majority owner of Bloomia, one of the largest producers of fresh-cut tulips in the United States, giving it a significant presence in the floriculture and value-added agri-products market.

Through this portfolio and operating structure, Bloomia targets growth in high-value crop segments while leveraging scale in production and distribution. Its strategy centers on using capital allocation and operational expertise to expand its footprint across both domestic and international markets within the specialty agriculture sector.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SES S.A. ( (FR:SESG) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 2, 2026, SES held its Annual General Meeting of shareholders in Betzdorf, Luxembourg, where investors approved all resolutions recommended by the board, including the 2025 annual accounts and a total annual dividend of EUR 0.50 per A-share and EUR 0.20 per B-share. The payout includes an interim dividend already distributed on October 16, 2025, and a final dividend scheduled for April 16, 2026, underscoring continued capital returns to shareholders.

Shareholders also backed governance measures, setting the board size at nine directors, re-electing Frank Esser and Anne-Catherine Ries, and appointing Joseph Cohen for a three-year term. The board re-elected Esser as chairperson and confirmed Ries and Peter van Bommel as vice-chairpersons for one year, reinforcing leadership continuity as SES advances its multi-orbit strategy and integration of Intelsat to drive verticalization and long-term value creation.

The most recent analyst rating on (FR:SESG) stock is a Hold with a EUR5.85 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SES S.A. stock, see the FR:SESG Stock Forecast page.

More about SES S.A.

SES S.A., headquartered in Luxembourg and listed on the Paris and Luxembourg stock exchanges under the ticker SESG, operates in the satellite communications and space connectivity industry. The company provides integrated multi-orbit satellite and terrestrial network solutions, delivering resilient connectivity and high-quality video services to governments, businesses, and consumers worldwide, and has expanded its scale and capabilities through the acquisition of Intelsat.

data.title: United Homes Group Amends Credit Facilities Amid Merger Plans
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Homes Group ( (UHG) ) has provided an update.

On March 31, 2026, United Homes Group, Inc. amended its Wells Fargo-led credit facility, securing temporary waivers of its debt service coverage and leverage ratio covenants until the earlier of May 31, 2026 or the occurrence of certain unrelated events of default. The amendment also obliges the company to fully refinance and repay this facility within 60 days if its pending merger with Stanley Martin Homes, LLC does not close by the outside date or is formally terminated.

On the same date, the company amended its Kennedy Lewis credit facility, waiving the debt service coverage and leverage ratio requirements for the period from January 1, 2026 through the fiscal quarter ending on or before March 31, 2026. That agreement likewise requires full refinancing and repayment within 60 days if the merger fails to close by the outside date, underscoring the extent to which United Homes Group’s capital structure and covenant relief are now closely tied to the completion of the Stanley Martin transaction.

The most recent analyst rating on (UHG) stock is a Sell with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on United Homes Group stock, see the UHG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on UHG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, UHG is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weakened financial performance—2025 losses, negative free cash flow, and elevated leverage—raising near-term risk. Technicals add pressure due to a sustained downtrend (price below key moving averages and negative MACD), while valuation provides limited offset because the negative P/E reflects loss-making conditions and there is no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on UHG stock, click here.

Village Farms International ( (VFF) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, Village Farms International’s long-serving Chief Financial Officer, Stephen Ruffini, notified the company that he intends to transition out of the CFO role as part of a planned succession process. He will remain in the position until a new CFO is appointed, and the company said his departure from the finance role is not linked to any disputes or concerns over financial reporting, accounting, operations, or policies.

Announced publicly on April 3, 2026, the succession plan will see Village Farms commence an immediate search for a new CFO while retaining Ruffini in a new executive post focused on mergers and acquisitions. The move underscores the company’s strategy to pair organic growth with accretive global acquisitions, building on Ruffini’s 17-year tenure in which he helped transform Village Farms into a leading cannabis CPG operator, complete key financings, and execute strategic transactions that supported its expansion and shareholder value.

To see Spark’s full report on VFF stock, click here.

More about Village Farms International

Village Farms International, Inc. is a global leader in cannabis and plant-based consumer packaged goods, leveraging decades of Controlled Environment Agriculture expertise and Dutch farming practices. The company operates extensive greenhouse and indoor cultivation assets, with major cannabis operations in Canada, participation in the regulated cannabis program in the Netherlands, and a large U.S. hemp-derived wellness platform.

Its Canadian operations include one of the world’s largest EU-GMP certified cannabis facilities and a leading portfolio of dried-flower brands such as Pure Sunfarms Pink Kush. Beyond cannabis, Village Farms runs a Clean Energy division that converts landfill gas into renewable natural gas and holds an equity stake in Vanguard Food LP, which is pursuing strategic acquisitions to build a North American branded food platform.

An update from Innate Pharma SA ( (FR:IPH) ) is now available.

Innate Pharma S.A., a clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in cancer immunotherapies and next-generation antibody therapeutics, is advancing a pipeline that includes IPH4502 for solid tumors, lacutamab for T cell lymphomas, and monalizumab in non-small cell lung cancer through collaborations with partners such as AstraZeneca and Sanofi. The company, headquartered in Marseille with a U.S. base in Rockville, is listed on both Euronext Paris and Nasdaq, underscoring its global reach in immuno-oncology.

On April 3, 2026, Innate Pharma announced that its executive team will hold one-on-one investor meetings at the Kempen Life Sciences Conference in Amsterdam on April 15–16, 2026. This investor-focused engagement signals continued efforts to strengthen relationships with the financial community, potentially increasing visibility of its oncology pipeline and supporting access to capital in a competitive biotech funding environment.

More about Innate Pharma SA

Innate Pharma S.A. is a global clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing immunotherapies for cancer patients. Leveraging antibody engineering and novel target identification, it is advancing a pipeline of next-generation antibody therapeutics, including Nectin-4 ADC IPH4502 for solid tumors, lacutamab for cutaneous and peripheral T cell lymphomas, and monalizumab, partnered with AstraZeneca, for non-small cell lung cancer.

The company operates through collaborations with major biopharmaceutical partners such as Sanofi and AstraZeneca, as well as academic institutions, to drive innovation in immuno-oncology. Headquartered in Marseille, France, with a U.S. office in Rockville, Maryland, Innate Pharma is dual-listed on Euronext Paris and Nasdaq under the tickers IPH and IPHA, respectively, reflecting its international capital markets footprint.

data.title: Atlantic International Challenges Creditor Default Claims in Court
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Atlantic International ( (ATLN) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, SPP Credit Advisors issued default notices to Atlantic International and its Lyneer subsidiaries under both an April 29, 2025 term loan agreement and a June 18, 2024 bridge loan, alleging breaches of financial and non-financial covenants. SPP reserved broad enforcement rights, attempted to exercise its collateral pledges and sought to replace Lyneer’s management, moves that Atlantic is contesting as baseless defaults designed to seize control of the Lyneer businesses.

Atlantic and the Lyneer subsidiaries responded on April 1, 2026 by filing suit in New York state court, seeking a temporary restraining order and preliminary injunction to block SPP from asserting management control, seizing assets or exercising loan remedies, arguing that SPP’s debt had been satisfied by over $77 million of Atlantic stock collateral and that its actions amount to a hostile takeover. The dispute has been further complicated by the March 30, 2026 resignation and for-cause termination of Chief Operating Officer Mathew Evelt, whom Atlantic accuses of egregious misconduct and of siding with SPP, raising governance concerns and legal uncertainty for lenders, shareholders and employees as the litigation proceeds.

The most recent analyst rating on (ATLN) stock is a Hold with a $3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Atlantic International stock, see the ATLN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ATLN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ATLN is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial health—loss-making operations and a highly concerning balance sheet with negative equity—despite strong revenue growth. Technicals are supportive with strong upward momentum above major moving averages, and corporate events are positive (acquisition and new CFO), but valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no stated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on ATLN stock, click here.

More about Atlantic International

Atlantic International Corp. operates through its Lyneer subsidiaries, including Lyneer Investments, Lyneer Holdings and Lyneer Staffing Solutions, which focus on staffing and workforce solutions. The group’s financing structure includes a term loan and a bridge loan provided by SPP Credit Advisors, secured in part by pledges over the ownership and governance rights of the Lyneer entities.

Average Trading Volume: 163,434

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $219.8M

For a thorough assessment of ATLN stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Galera Sets Deadlines for 2026 Combined Annual Meeting
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Galera Therapeutics ( (GRTX) ) has issued an update.

Galera Therapeutics has scheduled its combined 2025 and 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders for May 8, 2026, after its 2024 annual meeting was held on February 24, 2025, and is updating key deadlines for shareholder participation as a result of the later date. The company has set April 13, 2026 as the cutoff for shareholders to submit director nominations, other proposals under its bylaws or universal proxy rules, and for proposals to be considered for inclusion in its proxy materials, providing a clear timetable for investors seeking to influence board composition or agenda items at the meeting.

These revised deadlines underscore the company’s adherence to Exchange Act and universal proxy requirements while signaling to shareholders the procedural steps needed to exercise their governance rights. The clarified timeline may affect how active and institutional investors plan engagement, proxy campaigns, or governance initiatives ahead of the 2026 combined meeting.

The most recent analyst rating on (GRTX) stock is a Hold with a $0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Galera Therapeutics stock, see the GRTX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GRTX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRTX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing operating losses, and limited evidence of sustainable cash generation), despite improved balance-sheet positioning in 2025. Technicals are relatively supportive with an uptrend and positive MACD, while valuation signals are unreliable due to likely non-operating earnings. The corporate event modestly helps by improving capital-structure flexibility but carries dilution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on GRTX stock, click here.

More about Galera Therapeutics

Galera Therapeutics, Inc. is a U.S.-based biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel therapeutics, though this notice centers on its corporate governance practices rather than its product pipeline or commercial operations.

data.description:
New insider activity at Everspin Technologies ( (MRAM) ) has taken place on April 2, 2026.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Everspin Technologies has seen notable insider activity, with top executives recently selling shares in the company. President & CEO Sanjeev Aggarwal sold 19,440 shares of Everspin Technologies stock in a transaction valued at $178,848. In a separate move, CFO William Earl Cooper sold 1,549 shares of the company’s stock, for total proceeds of $14,250. These insider sales highlight recent profit-taking at the highest levels of Everspin’s leadership team.

Recent Updates on MRAM stock

Everspin shares reacted to Q4 results showing double-digit revenue growth led by MRAM product sales, but modestly softer EPS as licensing and other non-product revenue declined and margins ticked down. Needham trimmed its price target as the quarter highlighted near-term earnings pressure, partner- and contract-driven revenue timing risks, and multi-year timelines for new high-density MRAM and NOR replacement ramps, even as long-term growth drivers and a solid balance sheet remain intact.

Spark’s Take on MRAM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MRAM is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by a strong balance sheet and positive cash generation, reinforced by generally constructive earnings-call momentum and stable guidance. These positives are tempered by a weak technical trend and profitability/valuation headwinds, with earnings still pressured versus prior years.

data.title: ImmunityBio Insider Makes Bold Multi-Million Dollar Bet on the Company’s Future
data.description:
New insider activity at ImmunityBio ( (IBRX) ) has taken place on April 2, 2026.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ImmunityBio’s founder, Executive Chairman, and Global Chief Scientific and Medical Officer, Patrick Soon-Shiong, has significantly boosted his stake in the company by purchasing 4,606,596 shares of ImmunityBio stock. This sizeable insider buy is valued at $24,967,750, underscoring strong internal confidence in the company’s future prospects.

Recent Updates on IBRX stock

ImmunityBio shares rebounded about 6–7% to near $7.92 amid mixed options sentiment after a prior ~24% drop tied to an FDA warning letter over allegedly misleading ANKTIVA promotion and inadequate risk disclosure. BTIG argued the letter doesn’t alter fundamentals and viewed the pullback as tied to a fixable promotional issue ahead of key 2H26 bladder cancer data.

Analyst stance stayed neutral overall, constrained by weak financial durability, but recent developments supported sentiment: the company secured $75M in additional non-dilutive royalty financing and saw insider debt converted to equity, moves aimed at strengthening the balance sheet while ANKTIVA’s global approvals and a positive IDMC power assessment for the QUILT-2.005 study reinforced its longer-term commercial and clinical outlook.

Spark’s Take on IBRX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IBRX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial fundamentals (large ongoing losses, heavy cash burn, and negative equity). Earnings-call updates provide a meaningful offset via strong ANKTIVA commercialization and expanding regulatory approvals, but near-term technical signals are still bearish and valuation remains hard to justify without profitability.
